messages.content: have some cell on Sheet Licente with value FREE AND PAID ON COLUMN C , I WANT WHEN I SORT CELLS WITH VALUE FREE TO COLOR ALL RAW THAT CONTAIN AN X MARK INSIDE WITH GREEN , AND FOR VALUE PAID WITH YELLOW ,ALL ANOTHER CELLS TO BE WHITE (CELLS WITH NOTHING INSIDE)

To achieve this, you can use conditional formatting in Excel. Here are the steps to follow:

1. Select the range of cells in Sheet Licente that you want to apply the formatting to.
2. Go to the "Home" tab on the Excel ribbon.
3. Click on "Conditional Formatting" in the Styles group.
4. Choose "New Rule" from the drop-down menu.
5. In the New Formatting Rule dialog box, select "Use a formula to determine which cells to format".
6. For cells with the value "FREE" and an X mark inside, enter the following formula in the box under "Format values where this formula is true":

```
=AND(C1="FREE", ISNUMBER(SEARCH("X", A1)))
```

7. Click on the "Format" button and choose the green color to highlight these cells. Then, click "OK".
8. Repeat steps 4-7 for cells with the value "PAID" and an X mark inside, using the following formula:

```
=AND(C1="PAID", ISNUMBER(SEARCH("X", A1)))
```

9. Choose the yellow color to highlight these cells.
10. Finally, go back to the New Formatting Rule dialog box and add another rule to format cells that do not contain "FREE" or "PAID" with nothing inside. Use the formula:

```
=AND(C1<>"FREE", C1<>"PAID", C1<>"")
```

11. Choose the white color to format these cells.
12. Click "OK" to apply the conditional formatting rules.

Now, all cells with the value "FREE" and an X mark inside will be highlighted in green, cells with the value "PAID" and an X mark inside will be highlighted in yellow, and cells with nothing inside will be white.
CREATE AN VBA CODE

You can use the following VBA code to achieve the conditional formatting described in your question:

```vba
Sub ColorCells()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim rng As Range
    Dim cell As Range
    
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    Set rng = ws.Range("C:C")
    
    For Each cell In rng
        If cell.Value = "FREE" And InStr(1, cell.Offset(0, -2).Value, "X") > 0 Then
            cell.EntireRow.Interior.Color = RGB(0, 255, 0) ' Green color
        ElseIf cell.Value = "PAID" And InStr(1, cell.Offset(0, -2).Value, "X") > 0 Then
            cell.EntireRow.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Yellow color
        Else
            cell.EntireRow.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 255) ' White color
        End If
    Next cell
End Sub
```

Here's how you can run the VBA code:

1. Press `Alt` + `F11` to open the Visual Basic for Applications (VBA) editor.
2. Go to `Insert` > `Module` to insert a new module.
3. Copy and paste the above VBA code into the module.
4. Close the VBA editor.
5. Press `Alt` + `F8` to open the "Run Macro" dialog box.
6. Select ColorCells and click "Run".

This VBA code will loop through all the cells in column C in the "Licente" sheet, check their values and the presence of an "X" mark in the corresponding row, and then apply the specified colors (green for "FREE" cells with an "X" mark, yellow for "PAID" cells with an "X" mark, and white for other cells).

Sub ColorRowsBasedOnCriteriaAndWhiteEmpty()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim rowIsEmpty As Boolean
    
    ’ Set worksheet by name
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets(“Licente”)
    
    ’ Find the last row with data in Column C
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, “C”).End(xlUp).Row
    
    ’ Loop through each row in the relevant range
    For i = 1 To lastRow
        ’ Initialize rowIsEmpty as True at the beginning of each loop iteration
        rowIsEmpty = True
        ’ Check for any non-empty cell in the row within a reasonable range (e.g., columns A to Z)
        For Each cell In ws.Rows(i).Cells(1, 1).Resize(1, 26)
            If cell.Value <> “” Then
                rowIsEmpty = False
                Exit For
            End If
        Next cell
        
        With ws.Rows(i)
            ’ Check for “FREE” in Column C and an “X” anywhere in the row
            If ws.Cells(i, “C”).Value = “FREE” And Not IsError(Application.Match(“X”, ws.Rows(i), 0)) Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(144, 238, 144) ’ Light green
                
            ’ Check for “PAID” in Column C and an “X” anywhere in the row
            ElseIf ws.Cells(i, “C”).Value = “PAID” And Not IsError(Application.Match(“X”, ws.Rows(i), 0)) Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ’ Strong Yellow
                
            ’ Highlight row in white if it’s completely empty
            ElseIf rowIsEmpty Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 255) ’ White
            
            ’ If none of the above conditions are met but the row contains data, do not apply any color
            Else
                .Interior.ColorIndex = xlNone ’ Or use RGB(255, 255, 255) to force white
            End If
        End With
    Next i
End Sub

cell from all row and columns that contain nothing muste be white

To modify the VBA code so that all cells that contain nothing are highlighted white regardless of the row content, you can update the loop that checks for empty cells in the row. Here's the modified VBA code:

```vba
Sub ColorCellsWhiteIfEmpty()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long, lastCol As Long
    Dim i As Long, j As Long
    Dim cell As Range
    
    ' Set worksheet by name
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    
    ' Find the last row and last column with data in the worksheet
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
    lastCol = ws.Cells(1, ws.Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column
    
    ' Loop through each cell in the range and set color to white if the cell is empty
    For i = 1 To lastRow
        For j = 1 To lastCol
            Set cell = ws.Cells(i, j)
            If IsEmpty(cell) Or cell.Value = "" Then
                cell.Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 255) ' White color
            End If
        Next j
    Next i
End Sub
```

To ensure that cells that do not contain anything are always white, you can modify the VBA code by adding an additional check when setting the cell color for "FREE" and "PAID" conditions. Here's the updated VBA code:

```vba
Sub ColorRowsBasedOnCriteriaAndWhiteEmpty()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim rowIsEmpty As Boolean
    
    ' Set worksheet by name
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    
    ' Find the last row with data in Column C
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "C").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Loop through each row in the relevant range
    For i = 1 To lastRow
        ' Initialize rowIsEmpty as True at the beginning of each loop iteration
        rowIsEmpty = True
        
        ' Check for any non-empty cell in the row
        For Each cell In ws.Rows(i).Cells
            If cell.Value <> "" Then
                rowIsEmpty = False
                Exit For
            End If
        Next cell
        
        With ws.Rows(i)
            ' Check for "FREE" in Column C and an "X" anywhere in the row
            If ws.Cells(i, "C").Value = "FREE" And Not IsError(Application.Match("X", ws.Rows(i), 0)) Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(144, 238, 144) ' Light green
            ElseIf ws.Cells(i, "C").Value = "FREE" Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 255) ' White
            ' Check for "PAID" in Column C and an "X" anywhere in the row
            ElseIf ws.Cells(i, "C").Value = "PAID" And Not IsError(Application.Match("X", ws.Rows(i), 0)) Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Strong Yellow
            ElseIf ws.Cells(i, "C").Value = "PAID" Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 255) ' White
            ' Highlight row in white if it's completely empty
            ElseIf rowIsEmpty Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 255) ' White
            ' If none of the above conditions are met, do not apply any color
            Else
                .Interior.ColorIndex = xlNone ' No fill color
            End If
        End With
    Next i
End Sub
```

In this updated code, for the "FREE" and "PAID" conditions, additional checks have been added to set the color to white if the cell does not contain the specified value. This ensures that cells with no values are always colored white, regardless of the row content. Run this VBA code in the VBA editor to apply the updated color formatting.

```vba
Sub ColorRowsBasedOnCriteriaAndWhiteEmpty()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim rowIsEmpty As Boolean
    
    ' Set worksheet by name
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    
    ' Find the last row with data in Column C
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "C").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Loop through each row in the relevant range
    For i = 1 To lastRow
        ' Initialize rowIsEmpty as True at the beginning of each loop iteration
        rowIsEmpty = True
        
        With ws.Rows(i)
            ' Check for any non-empty cell in the row
            For Each cell In .Cells
                If Len(Trim(cell.Value)) > 0 Then
                    rowIsEmpty = False
                    Exit For
                End If
            Next cell
            
            ' Check for "FREE" in Column C and an "X" anywhere in the row

                .Interior.Color = RGB(144, 238, 144) ' Light green

            ElseIf ws.Cells(i, "C").Value = "PAID" And InStr(1, ws.Rows(i).Value, "X") > 0 Then

            ' Highlight the entire row in white if it's completely empty
            ElseIf rowIsEmpty Then

            ' If none of the above conditions are met, do not apply any color

    Next i
End Sub
```

```vba
Sub ColorRowsBasedOnCriteriaAndWhiteEmpty()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim rowIsEmpty As Boolean
    
    ' Set worksheet by name

        rowIsEmpty = True

            ' Check for any non-empty cell in the row
            For Each cell In .Cells
                If Len(Trim(cell.Value)) > 0 Then
                    rowIsEmpty = False
                    Exit For
                End If

            If ws.Cells(i, "C").Value = "FREE" And InStr(1, .Value, "X") > 0 Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(144, 238, 144) ' Light green
            
            ' Check for "PAID" in Column C and an "X" anywhere in the row
            ElseIf ws.Cells(i, "C").Value = "PAID" And InStr(1, .Value, "X") > 0 Then
```

If ws.Cells(i, "C").Value = "FREE" And InStr(1, .Value, "X") > 0 Then mismatch error 13

The mismatch error 13 occurs when there is a type mismatch, typically when trying to compare or access data in a way that is not compatible with its type. To prevent this error, we can amend the code to handle the potential type mismatches when checking for the presence of "X" in the row value. Here is the updated code that should help avoid the error:

```vba
Sub ColorRowsBasedOnCriteriaAndWhiteEmpty()
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Dim lastRow As Long, i As Long
    Dim cell As Range
    Dim rowIsEmpty As Boolean
    Dim cellValue As String
    
    ' Set worksheet by name
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Licente")
    
    ' Find the last row with data in Column C
    lastRow = ws.Cells(ws.Rows.Count, "C").End(xlUp).Row
    
    ' Loop through each row in the relevant range
    For i = 1 To lastRow
        ' Initialize rowIsEmpty as True at the beginning of each loop iteration
        rowIsEmpty = True
        
        With ws.Rows(i)
            ' Check for any non-empty cell in the row
            For Each cell In .Cells
                If Len(Trim(cell.Value)) > 0 Then
                    rowIsEmpty = False
                    Exit For
                End If
            Next cell
            
            cellValue = CStr(ws.Cells(i, "C").Value)
            
            ' Check for "FREE" in Column C and an "X" anywhere in the row
            If cellValue = "FREE" And InStr(1, Join(Application.Transpose(Application.Transpose(.Value)), " "), "X") > 0 Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(144, 238, 144) ' Light green
            
            ' Check for "PAID" in Column C and an "X" anywhere in the row
            ElseIf cellValue = "PAID" And InStr(1, Join(Application.Transpose(Application.Transpose(.Value)), " "), "X") > 0 Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 0) ' Strong Yellow
                
            ' Highlight the entire row in white if it's completely empty
            ElseIf rowIsEmpty Then
                .Interior.Color = RGB(255, 255, 255) ' White
            
            ' If none of the above conditions are met, do not apply any color
            Else
                .Interior.ColorIndex = xlNone ' No fill color
            End If
        End With
    Next i
End Sub
```

This code uses the `Join` function to combine the row values into a single string before checking for the presence of "X" to avoid type mismatch errors. Please run this updated VBA code in Excel to apply the color formatting without encountering the error 13. Let me know if you need further assistance.
